institution: University of North Carolina at Charlotte

ai_strategy_lead.name: Stephanie Schuckers
ai_strategy_lead.title: Co-Director, Charlotte AI Institute for Research; Bank of America Distinguished Professor in Computing and Informatics

documents.title: AI Taskforce Report (AI Compass: Teach / Learn / Govern / Grow)
Launching a New Era of Innovation: The Charlotte AI Institute for Research Strategic Plan 2026-2033
Chancellor/Provost statement on UNC Charlotte's Commitment to Responsible AI Use

ai_strategy.summary: UNC Charlotte has moved from distributed AI activity to a more coordinated posture anchored by two strategic documents: the August 2025 AI Taskforce Report (the 'AI Compass' organized around Teach / Learn / Govern / Grow) and the Charlotte AI Institute for Research Strategic Plan 2026-2033 released to accompany the institute's February 2025 launch. Operational execution sits with the Office of the Provost (AI Accelerator in Teaching and Learning, announced February 2026), OneIT (enterprise tools, AI Steering Committee, Student AI Fellows), and the Division of Research (Charlotte AI Institute and its eight AI-focused centers). The strategy explicitly positions the university as one of few R1s offering dedicated AI bachelor's and master's degrees alongside the UNC System's first AI-and-humanities major starting fall 2026.

key_provisions: Faculty set course-level AI permissions and are encouraged to include syllabus language on AI use
Students remain the primary author of their work even when AI use is permitted
Users must not put private or sensitive data into AI tools; enterprise versions with data protection are required for institutional data
OneIT AI Steering Committee owns guiding principles, software guidance, and a security checklist
Three division-level working groups (Academic Affairs, Research, Business Affairs) address AI practice in their domains
AI Compass (Taskforce Report) organizes institutional commitments around Teach, Learn, Govern, Grow
ai_policy.summary: UNC Charlotte's AI policy is a comprehensive framework rather than a single top-down mandate: the Chancellor/Provost's August 2024 responsible-AI statement defines principles, OneIT's AI Steering Committee operationalizes software guidance and security review, and the August 2025 AI Taskforce Report codifies the institutional 'AI Compass.' Faculty retain syllabus-level discretion, but data-privacy, ethics, and enterprise-tool requirements are institution-wide.

bodies.name: OneIT AI Steering Committee
bodies.scope: Guides responsible and ethical use and adoption of AI at UNC Charlotte; owns guiding principles, software guidance, and security checklist

bodies.name: Artificial Intelligence in Teaching and Learning Task Force
bodies.scope: Provost-convened task force (fall 2024) that produced the August 2025 AI Taskforce Report and 'AI Compass'; co-chaired by Kiran Budhrani and Manuel Perez-Quinones

bodies.name: AI Accelerator in Teaching and Learning
bodies.scope: Cross-divisional academic leadership body (12-member, launched February 2026) that operationalizes Taskforce recommendations; aligns curriculum and instructional practice across colleges

bodies.name: Charlotte AI Institute for Research (CLTAI2)
bodies.scope: Division of Research institute (launched February 3, 2025) coordinating eight AI centers, internal funding, and external-funding advocacy across disciplines

bodies.name: Academic Affairs AI Working Group
bodies.scope: Division working group with Faculty Council representation addressing AI practice in academic affairs

bodies.name: Division of Research AI Working Group
bodies.scope: Division working group addressing AI practice in research operations

bodies.name: Division of Business Affairs AI Working Group
bodies.scope: Division working group addressing AI practice in business operations

governance_structure.summary: Governance is layered across multiple axes. OneIT's AI Steering Committee owns enterprise-tool vetting and security posture; the Provost-convened AI Teaching and Learning Task Force (2024-2025) produced the AI Compass strategy and was succeeded in February 2026 by the 12-member AI Accelerator, a cross-divisional academic leadership body that moves Taskforce recommendations to action. The Division of Research's Charlotte AI Institute (CLTAI2), launched February 3, 2025, coordinates research centers, internal funding, and external-grant advocacy. No dedicated Chief AI Officer role exists as of April 2026, but three division-level AI working groups complement the cross-divisional structure.

initiatives.title: Charlotte AI Institute for Research (CLTAI2) launch
initiatives.description: UNC Charlotte launched the Charlotte AI Institute on February 3, 2025, days before receiving R1 Carnegie classification. Co-directed by Stephanie Schuckers (Bank of America Distinguished Professor, CCI) and George Banks (Belk College of Business), the institute coordinates eight AI-focused research centers, internal funding, external-funding advocacy, and industry partnerships.
initiatives.significance: The institute consolidates previously fragmented AI research under one R1-aligned banner and creates a single point of engagement for industry, government, and nonprofit partners — a model other emerging R1s are watching.

investment.funding_source: UNC Charlotte Division of Research with external and industry partners
investment.description: Cross-disciplinary research institute coordinating eight AI centers; strategic plan 2026-2033 released 2026.

collaborations.description: Connector to regional industry, extending founding partnerships of the School of Data Science.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates a formal research-governance layer that complements the Provost-side AI governance.

initiatives.title: AI Taskforce Report and 'AI Compass' (Teach / Learn / Govern / Grow)
initiatives.description: The Artificial Intelligence in Teaching and Learning Task Force, co-chaired by Kiran Budhrani and Manuel Perez-Quinones and convened by Provost Troyer in fall 2024, released its report on September 30, 2025, with input from 418 campus community members. The 'AI Compass' establishes four foundational commitments and recommends governance bodies, syllabus-policy updates, college-level advisory committees, AI-tool access expansion, and campus-wide training.
initiatives.significance: One of the most explicit philosophy-plus-recommendations documents produced by an R1 public university; functions as the institutional AI constitution rather than a narrow policy.

investment.description: Task-force and associated working groups, supported by Center for Teaching and Learning.

partners: AAC&U 2025 Institute on AI, Pedagogy and the Curriculum
collaborations.description: Charlotte's selection for AAC&U's 2025 institute informed Task-force philosophy.

initiatives.policy_implications: Directs all academic units to adopt the AI Compass and informs syllabus-level guidance, governance structures, and training priorities.

initiatives.title: AI Accelerator in Teaching and Learning (cross-divisional academic leadership body)
initiatives.description: Announced February 24, 2026, the AI Accelerator is a 12-member cross-divisional body — including Provost Jennifer Troyer, Associate Provost Teresa Petty, CTL Director Kiran Budhrani, and CS Professor Gabriel Terejanu — charged with moving the Taskforce's broad recommendations to actionable strategies, aligning institutional strategy with emerging AI curriculum, and supporting the fall-2026 AI-degree launches.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates a governance pattern where an R1 converts a task-force report into a standing, named implementation body rather than letting the report stall.

investment.description: Standing cross-divisional body with named members.

partners: AAC&U Institute on AI, Pedagogy and the Curriculum
collaborations.description: Builds on Charlotte's 2025 AAC&U institute participation.

initiatives.policy_implications: Aligns academic-unit AI activity with institutional strategy and curriculum.

initiatives.title: Bachelor's and Master's degrees in Artificial Intelligence (first in UNC System for MS)
initiatives.description: The College of Computing and Informatics will launch a BS and MS in Artificial Intelligence in fall 2026. The BS emphasizes core mathematics, computing, AI algorithms, AI software lifecycle, and ethics; the MS covers planning, design, implementation, testing, and management of AI systems. UNC Charlotte will be the first in the UNC System to offer a dedicated AI master's and one of two in the system to offer a focused AI bachelor's (alongside NC A&T).
initiatives.significance: Positions UNC Charlotte in an elite national cohort (~20 universities with a dedicated AI undergraduate degree, ~35 with a dedicated AI master's) and reinforces the Carolinas' AI workforce pipeline.

investment.funding_source: UNC Charlotte / College of Computing and Informatics
investment.description: New degree programs with dedicated curriculum and faculty.

partners: Charlotte-region employers in finance, energy, healthcare
collaborations.description: Curriculum informed by regional workforce demand.

initiatives.title: AI and Humanities interdisciplinary program (first in UNC System)
initiatives.description: UNC Charlotte is launching a new AI and humanities major and minor in fall 2026 under the College of Humanities & Earth and Social Sciences (CHESS), Office of Interdisciplinary Studies, directed by Justin Cary. Students would earn an interdisciplinary-studies degree with an AI specialization combining humanities, social sciences, and applied technology.
initiatives.significance: Only UNC System institution to offer an AI-humanities curriculum; a public counter-example to narratives that AI degrees must live in computing or business colleges.

investment.funding_source: UNC Charlotte / College of Humanities & Earth and Social Sciences
investment.description: New interdisciplinary degree path with AI specialization.

collaborations.description: Partnership across humanities, social sciences, and technology.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot with Data Protection campus-wide deployment
initiatives.description: OneIT launched Microsoft Copilot with Data Protection on July 26, 2024, available to students, faculty, and staff campus-wide as a secure alternative to consumer-oriented generative AI services. Users sign in with their @Charlotte.edu account and see a green shield indicating enterprise data protection.
initiatives.significance: Makes enterprise-grade generative AI table-stakes for ~32,000 students and all employees, and pre-dates the system-wide enterprise-Copilot rollouts occurring at other UNC System campuses.

investment.description: Campus-wide Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat with commercial data protection.

collaborations.description: Enterprise licensing for Copilot with data protection.

initiatives.policy_implications: Establishes the official enterprise AI tool for handling any non-public data.

initiatives.title: Multi-tool enterprise AI portfolio (Gemini, NotebookLM, Zoom AI Companion)
initiatives.description: In addition to Copilot, OneIT now provides campus-wide access to Google Gemini (with Data Protection), Google NotebookLM, Zoom AI Companion, and Adobe creative-AI tools (labs / faculty / staff). The AI Hub (aihub.charlotte.edu) consolidates tool access, software guidance, and security documentation for all approved platforms.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates a multi-vendor enterprise posture rather than a single-vendor lock-in, contrasting with peer R1s that default to a single platform.

investment.description: Enterprise licensing across multiple AI vendors.

tools_and_vendors.name: Adobe AI creative tools
tools_and_vendors.role: Creative-suite AI features in labs and for employees

initiatives.policy_implications: OneIT AI Software Guidance specifies which tools are approved for which data classes.

initiatives.title: Student AI Fellows Program
initiatives.description: OneIT's Student AI Fellows Program, announced April 9, 2026, will recruit and train undergraduate students as embedded AI Fellows paired with campus departments. Target: 25 credentialed Fellows and 25 completed departmental projects by end of FY 2026-27. Training includes the Google AI Essentials Certificate and institutional responsible-AI guidelines; only approved tools (Gemini, Copilot, NotebookLM) are permitted.
initiatives.significance: A student-labor model for GenAI enablement that bridges AI literacy, workforce readiness, and operational adoption — transferable to other institutions trying to scale responsible AI use.

investment.description: Pilot in summer 2026 with academic-year expansion.

collaborations.description: Third-party certification embedded in student training.

initiatives.policy_implications: Explicitly operates within Charlotte's responsible-AI guidelines.

initiatives.title: AI4Health, TAIMing AI, CIPHER, and five additional AI research centers
initiatives.description: Under the Charlotte AI Institute for Research umbrella: AI4Health (personalized health care), Center for Trustworthy AI through Model Risk Management (TAIMing AI), Center for Humane AI Studies (CHAIS), CIPHER (computational intelligence for health and environmental risks), Center for Applied GIScience (CAGIS), CESAR (energy and cybersecurity), Center for Leadership Science, and CITeR (industry/government challenges, pending NSF approval).
initiatives.significance: A diversified research portfolio that deliberately spans technical, applied, humanistic, and risk-management AI — one of the broader center portfolios among R1 newcomers.

investment.funding_source: Mix of NSF, industry, state, and internal funds
investment.description: Portfolio of AI research centers under the Charlotte AI Institute.

collaborations.description: CIPHER explicitly partners with NC State and NC A&T.

initiatives.policy_implications: TAIMing AI produces model-risk-management frameworks used in institutional AI governance.

initiatives.title: School of Data Science (Carolinas' first) and PhD in Data Science launch
initiatives.description: The School of Data Science (SDS) — described as the Carolinas' first — is a five-college collaboration (Belk College of Business, College of Computing and Informatics, College of Health and Human Services, College of Humanities & Earth and Social Sciences, William States Lee College of Engineering) offering BS in Data Science, BS in Sports Analytics, MS in Data Science and Business Analytics, MS in Health Informatics and Analytics, a graduate certificate, and a PhD in Data Science launching fall 2025.
initiatives.significance: Cements the multidisciplinary data-science pipeline that feeds the AI degree programs and Charlotte AI Institute research.

investment.description: SDS raised more than $100,000 through Founding Members program to support education and research.

collaborations.description: Founding industry partners of the School of Data Science.

initiatives.title: NSF $5M SPARC 6 grant to expand STEM pathways (including data science and CS)
initiatives.description: Announced March 2026, UNC Charlotte received $2M of a $5M NSF grant under the SPARC 6 initiative — a five-institution collaboration with four North Carolina community colleges — to provide scholarships and improve retention, transfer, and graduation in biology, computer science, and data science.
initiatives.significance: Pipes historically underrepresented community-college transfer students into the AI/data-science programs, connecting AI workforce development to equity goals.

investment.description: $5M total NSF award; $2M to UNC Charlotte.

initiatives.title: NSF Regional Innovation Engines finalist (Charlotte energy/AI region)
initiatives.description: UNC Charlotte was named a finalist in NSF's highly competitive Regional Innovation Engines program, positioning the Charlotte region as a national energy and AI innovation hub. If awarded, the region could see up to $160 million in federal investment plus billions in potential private capital.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates Charlotte's rising stature in federal research-and-innovation programs and links AI to regional economic development.

investment.description: Up to $160M federal investment if awarded, plus leveraged private investment.

collaborations.description: Finalist status for NSF Engines program with NCInnovation-funded readiness work.

initiatives.title: AI Summit for Smarter Learning (signature annual event)
initiatives.description: The Charlotte AI Summit for Smarter Learning has become UNC Charlotte's signature campus-wide AI professional development event, run by the Center for Teaching and Learning, with 2025 and 2026 editions. Brings faculty and academic staff together to share practice-based approaches to AI in pedagogy, assessment, and discipline-specific use.
initiatives.significance: A model for campus-wide AI literacy programming that's more structured than one-off workshops and cheaper than formal fellowships.

partners: AAC&U 2025 Institute on AI, Pedagogy and the Curriculum

initiatives.title: Starlight research cluster (HPC + GPU partition)
initiatives.description: OneIT's University Research Computing operates the Starlight research cluster, a Slurm-based Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 HPC environment with the Orion general partition (mix of Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC nodes) and a dedicated GPU partition for faculty-sponsored research projects. Supports both research and educational clusters.
initiatives.significance: R1-scale research computing capacity is a prerequisite for competitive AI research funding.

initiatives.title: Professional / continuing education AI credentials
initiatives.description: The School of Professional Studies offers a Professional Certificate in Generative AI Tools and an online AI Prompting program through Ziplines, extending AI skilling beyond matriculated students to working professionals in the Charlotte region.
initiatives.significance: An executive-education channel for AI reskilling in the Charlotte workforce.

computing_infrastructure.summary: UNC Charlotte's University Research Computing operates the Starlight cluster — a Slurm-managed RHEL 9 HPC environment with the Orion general partition (Intel Xeon + AMD EPYC nodes) and a dedicated GPU partition for faculty-sponsored projects. OneIT also maintains a separate educational cluster for teaching. Specific GPU counts and accelerator model are not disclosed publicly.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: SPARC 6 community-college transfer pathway (biology, CS, data science)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: $2M NSF-funded UNC Charlotte share of $5M grant providing scholarships and retention/transfer supports for academically talented, low-income community-college students entering data-heavy STEM fields.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Enterprise AI tools available to all students
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: OneIT provides campus-wide access to Copilot (data protection), Gemini, NotebookLM, Zoom AI Companion, and Adobe tools at no additional cost to students, reducing the premium-tool access gap.

equity_and_access_initiatives.description: CHESS interdisciplinary major and minor provides a humanities-based entry point to AI study for students whose path does not include a CS-heavy curriculum.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Women in Data Science (WiDS) Charlotte Conference
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Annual campus and regional conference advancing women in data science and AI.

regulatory_context.summary: North Carolina Governor Josh Stein signed an executive order (2025) establishing the NC AI Leadership Council, an AI Accelerator within NCDIT, and AI Oversight Teams at every state agency. Five higher-education leaders (including Dr. Stan Ahalt from UNC-Chapel Hill's School of Data Science and Society) sit on the Leadership Council, tying UNC System institutions to state AI governance. Governor Cooper's earlier Executive Order 24 (2024) on 'Advancing Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence' laid the groundwork. The UNC System itself has not issued a single standardized AI policy; each campus (including UNC Charlotte) sets its own guidance, though UNC System-level Faculty Learning Communities exist on AI teaching.

narrative_summary: UNC Charlotte enters the 2026 academic year as a newly designated R1 (February 13, 2025) that has moved faster than most peers at reorganizing for an AI-native future. The institution's posture is coordinated across three clear axes. On governance, the Provost's Artificial Intelligence in Teaching and Learning Task Force — co-chaired by Kiran Budhrani and Manuel Perez-Quinones — produced the 'AI Compass' report (September 30, 2025) organized around Teach, Learn, Govern, and Grow; the 12-member AI Accelerator (February 24, 2026) now operationalizes those recommendations. A second axis is the Charlotte AI Institute for Research (CLTAI2), launched February 3, 2025 and co-directed by Stephanie Schuckers and George Banks, which coordinates eight AI-focused research centers spanning health (AI4Health, CIPHER), trustworthy AI (TAIMing AI), humanities (CHAIS), geoscience (CAGIS), energy and cybersecurity (CESAR), leadership science, and a pending NSF CITeR. The third axis is enterprise tooling under CIO Mike Carlin's OneIT: Microsoft Copilot with Data Protection went live campus-wide in July 2024, followed by Gemini, NotebookLM, Zoom AI Companion, and Adobe tools, all vetted by an AI Steering Committee that publishes guiding principles, a security checklist, and software guidance. Academic investment is unusually aggressive for a mid-size public: UNC Charlotte will be the first UNC System school to offer a dedicated AI master's and one of two to offer a dedicated AI bachelor's (fall 2026), alongside the UNC System's first AI-and-humanities major and a PhD in Data Science (fall 2025). Industry ties run through the Carolinas' first School of Data Science with founding partners Bank of America, Duke Energy, Lowe's, Wells Fargo, TIAA, and Sia Partners. Equity is approached via a $2M NSF SPARC 6 grant creating community-college pathways and via universal enterprise-AI access for students. Gaps: no standalone Chief AI Officer role, no disclosed GPU counts on the Starlight HPC cluster, and state-level AI policy is still more UNC-Chapel Hill-heavy than UNC Charlotte-heavy.
